rows.finalText: On "What is the next right step in my calling", this 3-card leans into purpose / calling themes with a right path emphasis. Past opens with Tower (19), putting you may need stronger standards and boundaries in focus and setting the emotional weather around the reading. Tower often looks like distance, standards, official structure, and the part of the situation governed by rules rather than mood in your calling. In Present, Snake (7) becomes the pivot, so strategy and layered motives shape outcomes turns into both pressure and opportunity. Snake colors your calling through layered motives, complexity, desire, and the need to read around what looks obvious. Looking ahead, Future with Stork (17) points toward a healthy update is ready, if the middle-card lesson is handled with care. Stork colors your calling through improvement, transition, upgrades, and the feeling that something is trying to move into a better form. Tower (19) with Stork (17) show what most shapes the question of calling: a blend of institution and change, asking you to balance professionalism with connection while staying alert to constant switching without consolidation. The spread turns on Snake (7) more than any other card. Snake colors your calling through layered motives, complexity, desire, and the need to read around what looks obvious. Tower (19) with Stork (17) keep emphasizing a blend of institution and change, asking you to balance professionalism with connection while staying alert to constant switching without consolidation. Separate signal from social noise so Stork (17) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
